name: netops-mcp
description: Network diagnosis that tells you whose side the problem is on — locally.
long_description:
netops-mcp runs network diagnostics on **your own machine**, so it sees what cloud probes structurally can't — your `/etc/hosts`, VPN routes, local resolvers and WireGuard. Instead of dumping raw `ping`/`dig`/`curl` output, it returns a **verdict**: which side the fault is on, yours or theirs, with the raw probe data underneath it.

Safe by default: read-only, no shell execution (every system call is `execFile` with an argv array), untrusted network output is wrapped before it reaches the model, and zero telemetry. The WireGuard write tools are flag-gated and dry-run unless you confirm.

tools.name: net_diagnose
tools.description: One-shot 'why can't I reach X' — DNS to HTTP, returns a verdict

tools.name: net_triangulate
tools.description: Is it me or them? Local probe vs Globalping worldwide

tools.name: diagnosis_bundle
tools.description: Full probe battery rendered as a shareable Markdown report

tools.name: config_correlate
tools.description: Cross-check /etc/hosts against live DNS for stale pins

tools.name: dns_lookup
tools.description: Resolve DNS records, optional custom resolver

tools.name: net_ping
tools.description: ICMP with TCP-ping fallback (no root needed)

tools.name: tcp_port_check
tools.description: Connectivity check of named ports (capped, not a scan)

tools.name: tls_inspect
tools.description: Cert chain, expiry, SANs, protocol/cipher, handshake timing

tools.name: mtu_blackhole
tools.description: Path-MTU discovery; catches MTU black holes

tools.name: cert_sweep
tools.description: TLS expiry across domains, auto-extracted from proxy configs

tools.name: tunnel_diff
tools.description: Direct vs tunnel egress identity — split-tunnel leak detection

tools.name: dns_leak_check
tools.description: Egress IP + resolvers actually used (leak heuristics)

tools.name: wg_peer_add
tools.description: Add/update a WireGuard peer (write-gated, dry-run by default)

tools.name: wg_peer_remove
tools.description: Remove a WireGuard peer (write-gated, dry-run by default)

local_only.title: Local-only mode
local_only.description: Disable all third-party calls (Globalping triangulation and the egress-IP echo). Everything else still works, fully offline.

enable_write.title: Allow WireGuard writes
enable_write.description: Permit the mutating WireGuard tools (wg_peer_add / wg_peer_remove). They still dry-run unless the call passes confirm:true. Leave off for a strictly read-only server.

allow.title: Allowlist (optional)
allow.description: Comma/space-separated hosts or CIDRs. When set, only these targets are allowed (strict mode).

deny.title: Denylist (optional)
deny.description: Comma/space-separated hosts or CIDRs to block.
